story: Air Force Enhanced Use Lease offering for private AI data centers at JBER, Eielson AFB, and Clear Space Force Station
window_note: RFLP issued April 10, 2026; proposal deadline June 29, 2026; no award/selection announced as of air date 2026-07-22. Pitched honestly as a pending decision, not breaking news.

safe_facts.claim: Land offered for AI data centers across 3 Alaska installations
safe_facts.value: ~4,700 acres, 12 parcels, Joint Base Elmendorf-Richardson + Eielson Air Force Base + Clear Space Force Station
Enhanced Use Lease (DAF leases non-excess land to a private partner for long-term commercial use; DAF receives at least fair market value)
reported by trade press as up to 50 years; NOT stated in the primary DAF release
safe_facts.confidence: medium - label on screen as reported, do not present as DAF-confirmed

safe_facts.value: June 29, 2026 (extended from an original May 29 date)
"This is a unique opportunity for a true public-private partnership."
safe_facts.source: Robert Moriarty, Deputy Assistant Secretary of the Air Force for Installations, af.mil 2026-04-10
safe_facts.confidence: high, verbatim confirmed by both validators

safe_facts.value: Sarah Hollister, whose family has a third-generation home on Eielson Farm Road near eligible land, on the noise: "it's like having a vacuum cleaner on 24 hours a day, seven days a week"; also raised permafrost/heat concerns ("data centers tend to let off a ton of heat, and what can that do to our permafrost?"), property values, and grid load
safe_facts.confidence: high, independently live-fetched and confirmed by validator 2

safe_facts.value: three of the proposed sites would draw on the Golden Valley Electric Association grid
safe_facts.source: Alaska's News Source, 2026-06-08 (reporter framing / resident concern, NOT a GVEA official statement)
safe_facts.confidence: medium - attribute to reporting/resident concern only, GVEA itself has made no public statement

safe_facts.value: No operator selection or award has been publicly announced as of 2026-07-22; proposals are presumably under evaluation
safe_facts.source: absence of any post-deadline announcement across af.mil, spaceforce.mil, and Alaska press

cut_do_not_use.claim: North Pole Middle School / "less than a mile" proximity framing
cut_do_not_use.reason: CONTESTED: validator 1 attributed it to the News-Miner; validator 2 independently fetched the News-Miner coverage and found no school content whatsoever, and traced the real Hollister quotes to a different outlet (Alaska's News Source). Unverifiable by the stricter live-fetch check. CUT per doctrine (unverifiable = cut).

cut_do_not_use.claim: "a constant hum" quote attributed to Sarah Hollister
cut_do_not_use.reason: Confirmed FABRICATED by validator 1 (does not appear in the source it was drawn from). CUT.

cut_do_not_use.claim: Ben Eielson Jr./Sr. High School building reuse
cut_do_not_use.reason: Only sourced to the same contested News-Miner article thread as the school-proximity claim; not independently re-confirmed. CUT for safety, not essential to the story.
Unverified by either validator against a live primary source. Do not cite a solicitation number on screen.

cut_do_not_use.claim: July 20-22 Eielson Industry Day (mixed-use EUL, 3,967 acres)
cut_do_not_use.reason: Validator 2 found this is a SEPARATE, unrelated mixed-use development lease (residential/commercial/office), not an update on the AI data center project. Do not conflate.

dedupe_entities: jber, eielson air force base, clear space force station, enhanced use lease, robert moriarty, sarah hollister, joint base elmendorf richardson, golden valley electric association
dedupe_check_result: FRESH (2026-07-22, confirmed distinct from Stak Energy North Slope and AIDEA Mat-Su prior dispatches)
